—This book does not mention David’s sin regarding Uriah, nor the troubles that followed it; they had no necessary connection with the subjects noted here. But David's sin in numbering the people is related: in the atonement made for that sin, there was notice of the place on which the temple should be built. The command to David to build an altar was a blessed token of reconciliation. God testified his acceptance of David’s offerings on this altar. Thus Christ was made sin and a curse for us; it pleased the Lord to bruise him, so that through him, God might be to us, not a consuming Fire, but a reconciled God. It is good to continue attending those ordinances in which we have experienced the tokens of God's presence, and have found that he is with us truly. Here God graciously met me, therefore I will still expect to meet him.
